Common_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Candidate engineering target to increase glycolytic phosphoenolpyruvate supply for shikimate and phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct AtMYB12 and SlMYB12 target in primary metabolism whose activation is associated with increased carbon flux through glycolysis and higher phenylpropanoid accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq, ChIP-qPCR, ChIP-seq, promoter scanning, peel/flesh qRT-PCR, isotope feeding/flux analysis.
Common_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Ripening fruit, especially 3–10 days after breaker; peel-specific endogenous expression in WT.
Common_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from RNA-seq of AtMYB12 fruit and validated as direct target by ChIP assays.
Common_Genes.Genetic_Population: AtMYB12 transgenic tomato vs WT; WT peel vs flesh.
Common_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: 14C and 13C glucose feeding for respiration/flux; LC-MS-based metabolite profiling in study.
Common_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Specifically described as plastidial enolase; links primary metabolism to aromatic amino acid-specialized metabolism interface through PEP supply.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GEO GSE61014; ChIP-seq GEO GSE62462
Common_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Associated with reduced sugar pools and increased aromatic amino acids in AtMYB12 fruit.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Primary metabolism reprogramming may affect sugar content and broader fruit composition.
Common_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: NA for gene alone; contributes to stronger whole-system flux in AtMYB12 fruit.

Common_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Possible target for carbohydrate partitioning to support engineered secondary metabolism, though evidence here is suggestive rather than definitive.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to altered sugar metabolism and carbon mobilization in AtMYB12 fruit.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq induction and ChIP-qPCR promoter enrichment without statistically definitive direct-binding evidence.
Common_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Study-wide sugar/metabolite profiling and isotope flux analyses.
Common_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Promoter enriched in ChIP but direct interaction not established with statistical significance; classified here as common carbohydrate-metabolism gene rather than core pathway enzyme/regulator.
Common_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Associated contextually with reduced major sugars in AtMYB12 fruit.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering sucrose metabolism could affect fruit sugar quality.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target for increasing phenylpropanoid/flavonoid accumulation through enhanced entry from phenylalanine.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamic acid, committing carbon from aromatic amino acids into general phenylpropanoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct AtMYB12 target associated with increased phenylpropanoid biosynthesis in engineered tomato fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-qPCR promoter enrichment in AtMYB12 fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Ripening fruit, several days after breaker.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Established annotation as PAL family gene; treated as known phenylpropanoid enzyme in study.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Promoter directly bound by AtMYB12; PAL step described as first reaction of general phenylpropanoid metabolism leading to p-coumaroyl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate gene identified from RNA-seq and ChIP analyses of AtMYB12 targets.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: LC-MS/HPLC-MS of flavonols and hydroxycinnamates/chlorogenic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: PAL5A, PAL5C, and PAL5D were all detected as bound promoters; isoform-specific metabolite effects were not separated.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GEO GSE61014; ChIP-seq GEO GSE62462 (study-wide)

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target for increasing phenylpropanoid/flavonoid accumulation through enhanced entry from phenylalanine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct AtMYB12 target associated with increased phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-qPCR promoter enrichment in AtMYB12 fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: PAL described as first step of general phenylpropanoid metabolism; PAL5C promoter directly bound by AtMYB12.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GEO GSE61014; ChIP-seq GEO GSE62462

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target for increasing phenylpropanoid/flavonoid accumulation through enhanced entry from phenylalanine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct AtMYB12 target associated with increased phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-qPCR promoter enrichment in AtMYB12 fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: PAL described as first step of general phenylpropanoid metabolism; PAL5D promoter directly bound by AtMYB12.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GEO GSE61014; ChIP-seq GEO GSE62462

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Key structural target for increasing flavonols or rerouting carbon to stilbenes/isoflavones depending on competing enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA units to form chalcone, the first committed flavonoid intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct AtMYB12 and SlMYB12 target promoting flavonoid accumulation; competed by VvStSy in transgenic fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-qPCR, ChIP-seq, promoter scanning, qRT-PCR, and metabolite changes in transgenic combinations.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Ripening fruit; peel-specific endogenous regulation also shown.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Known flavonoid enzyme; used as canonical AtMYB12 target.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Loss/competition context shown by VvStSy strongly diverting flux away from flavonols; promoter directly bound by AtMYB12 and SlMYB12.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known AtMYB12 target validated in tomato ChIP assays.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: AtMYB12 transgenic versus WT; WT peel versus flesh; AtMYB12 × VvStSy combinations.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Stilbene synthase competed effectively with CHS, reducing high flavonol accumulation in AtMYB12 StSy tomatoes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GEO GSE61014; ChIP-seq GEO GSE62462
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competition with stilbene synthase can reduce flavonol output.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Key branch-control point; reducing its activity can redirect flux from flavonols to isoflavones in engineered tomato.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones such as naringenin to dihydroflavonols.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: 2-oxoglutarate, Fe2+, O2, ascorbate typical for 2-ODD enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct AtMYB12 target required for flavonol biosynthesis; reduced activity in are mutant decreases flavonols and increases isoflavones when LjIFS is present.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-qPCR promoter enrichment; qRT-PCR; natural are mutant analysis in transgenic combinations with LjIFS and AtMYB12.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Known flavonoid enzyme; are mutant described as deficient in F3H activity.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Promoter directly bound by AtMYB12; are mutant reduces flavonol production to ~30% of AtMYB12 line and redirects naringenin to IFS.
Pathway_Genes.Favorable_Allele: For flavonols: functional F3H; for isoflavone engineering: are-/- reduced-function background
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known MYB12 target; functional contribution evaluated using are mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: AtMYB12 transgenic tomato; AtMYB12 are-/-; segregating F2 with LjIFS.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: are mutation, exact lesion not specified in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: IFS competes directly with F3H for naringenin substrate; reduced F3H sharply increases genistin/isoflavone accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Reduced flavonol accumulation in are background.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower F3H decreases beneficial flavonols while increasing isoflavones.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: AtMYB12 are-/- fruit had only ~30% of the flavonols of AtMYB12 fruit; in LjIFS background isoflavones increased to ~78 mg g^-1 DW in are-/- lines.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Target for enhanced flavonol biofortification downstream of AtMYB12/SlMYB12 activation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dihydroflavonols to flavonols by desaturation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Known AtMYB12 target associated with increased flavonol accumulation in tomato peel and transgenic fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression analysis and prior known-target status cited in paper.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Ripening fruit; peel at 5 days after breaker.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Cited as known AtMYB12 target; peel expression correlates with SlMYB12 expression.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known candidate target examined by qRT-PCR.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: WT peel versus flesh; AtMYB12 transgenic context.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The Results text cites FLS as a previously demonstrated AtMYB12 target rather than newly ChIP-validated here.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Important engineering target to boost precursor supply for phenylpropanoids, aromatic amino acids, and high-value specialized metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and erythrose-4-phosphate to form 3-deoxy-D-arabino-heptulosonate 7-phosphate, the first committed step of shikimate pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Divalent metal ion typically required; not specified in paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct AtMYB12 and SlMYB12 target that increases carbon flux into shikimate pathway and supports aromatic amino acid/phenylpropanoid production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq upregulation, ChIP-qPCR, ChIP-seq, promoter scanning, peel/flesh qRT-PCR, isotope flux analysis, and metabolite profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Ripening tomato fruit; peel in WT and fruit pericarp in AtMYB12 lines.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Known shikimate pathway entry enzyme; described as key determinant of carbon flow into shikimate pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Direct promoter binding by AtMYB12/SlMYB12 and established pathway position as entry enzyme for aromatic amino acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Highlighted from RNA-seq/ChIP as primary-metabolism target of AtMYB12.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: AtMYB12 transgenic tomato vs WT; WT peel vs flesh.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: 13C/14C glucose feeding flux analysis; amino acid LC-MS; phenylpropanoid LC-MS.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes DAHPS as a key determinant linking primary metabolism to specialized metabolism of aromatic amino acids.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq GEO GSE61014; ChIP-seq GEO GSE62462
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Associated with increased aromatic amino acids and reduced sugars in AtMYB12 fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry into shikimate/aromatic amino acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad reprogramming of primary metabolism may reduce major sugars.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: NA for gene alone; contributes to strong whole-fruit phenylpropanoid enhancement under AtMYB12.
Pathway_Genes.Target_Metabolite_Product: Phenylpropanoid compounds via aromatic amino acid supply

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Candidate target to increase erythrose-4-phosphate and reducing power for aromatic amino acid and phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to increased OPPP flux and precursor/reducing power supply in AtMYB12 fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq induction and ChIP-qPCR promoter enrichment without full statistical support for direct interaction.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Gene grouped among OPPP enzymes elevated in AtMYB12 fruit; promoter enriched in ChIP.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: 14C/13C glucose flux analysis at pathway level.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Paper states direct binding not established with statistical significance despite enrichment.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Candidate target to support aromatic amino acid and phenylpropanoid precursor generation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Interconverts ribose-5-phosphate and ribulose-5-phosphate in pentose phosphate metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to enhanced pentose phosphate pathway activity in AtMYB12 fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq induction and ChIP-qPCR promoter enrichment without definitive direct-binding significance.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Grouped among primary metabolism genes elevated by AtMYB12; promoter enriched in ChIP.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Direct binding remained suggestive rather than statistically conclusive.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Candidate target to support carbon flow into aromatic amino acid and phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves/fructose-bisphosphate interconverts trioses in glycolysis (exact aldolase reaction not specified).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to increased glycolytic flux in AtMYB12 fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq induction and ChIP-qPCR promoter enrichment without definitive direct-binding significance.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Among glycolytic genes elevated by AtMYB12 and enriched in ChIP.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Direct target status was suggestive but not statistically confirmed.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Candidate target for boosting aromatic amino acid supply and downstream phenylpropanoid production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely supports increased shikimate pathway flux in AtMYB12 fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq induction and ChIP-qPCR promoter enrichment without definitive direct-binding significance.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Among shikimate genes elevated by AtMYB12 and enriched in ChIP.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: 13C/14C flux analysis and amino acid/metabolite profiling at pathway level.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Promoter enrichment observed but direct interaction not statistically secured.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Candidate target for improving aromatic amino acid supply to secondary metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely supports increased shikimate pathway flux and precursor supply in AtMYB12 fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq induction and ChIP-qPCR promoter enrichment without definitive direct-binding significance.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Among shikimate genes elevated by AtMYB12 and enriched in ChIP.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: 13C/14C flux analysis and metabolite profiling at pathway level.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Promoter enrichment observed but direct interaction not statistically secured.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Candidate target for broad aromatic amino acid enhancement under AtMYB12 regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers phosphoribosyl group in the anthranilate-to-tryptophan pathway (exact direction not detailed in paper).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to elevated aromatic amino acids in AtMYB12 fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq induction and ChIP-qPCR promoter enrichment without definitive direct-binding significance.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Annotated aromatic amino acid biosynthetic enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Grouped among AtMYB12-upregulated primary metabolism genes with promoter enrichment in ChIP; aromatic amino acids Trp and Tyr increased in AtMYB12 fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Amino acid LC-MS and pathway-level flux analyses.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Although not directly on phenylalanine branch, reflects broader aromatic amino acid reprogramming by AtMYB12.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Associated with increased Trp/Tyr pools under AtMYB12 context.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Tryptophan branch of aromatic amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Target_Metabolite_Class: aromatic amino acid / phenylpropanoid support metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Target_Metabolite_Product: Phenylpropanoids indirectly via aromatic amino acid network

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Structural gene for engineering resveratrol and derivatives at high levels in tomato fruit, especially when combined with AtMYB12.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form resveratrol/stilbene skeleton, competing with chalcone synthase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers resveratrol and glycosylated derivative accumulation in tomato fruit; AtMYB12 co-expression greatly enhances output and reduces flavonol accumulation by competition with CHS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic 35S:VvStSy expression, genetic crosses with AtMYB12, LC-MS quantification of resveratrol derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Transgenic expression produced resveratrol and derivatives in tomato fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Competition with CHS and production of resveratrol/polydatin demonstrate placement at diversion from flavonoid branch.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Heterologous candidate structural gene from grape introduced transgenically.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Original 35S:VvStSy tomato line and crosses with AtMYB12; also are background tested.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening; transgene co-expression with AtMYB12.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: LC-MS/HPLC-MS of resveratrol, polydatin, and derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: AtMYB12 StSy fruit lost the high flavonol phenotype seen with AtMYB12 alone, indicating strong competition of StSy with CHS.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Reduced flavonol accumulation relative to AtMYB12 line.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Stilbene branch diverging from p-coumaroyl-CoA/malonyl-CoA module
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Diverts flux away from endogenous flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Original 35S:VvStSy fruit produced ~0.5 mg g^-1 DW trans-resveratrol; with AtMYB12 total resveratrol and derivatives reached ~5–6 mg g^-1 DW, driven largely by 4–5-fold increase in polydatin; methylated resveratrol derivative ~1 mg g^-1 DW detected.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Structural gene for engineering genistein/genistin-rich tomato fruit, especially when combined with AtMYB12 and reduced F3H activity.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts flavanone substrate such as naringenin into isoflavone skeleton leading to genistein; product is subsequently glycosylated to genistin.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase system with NADPH and O2 typically required; not detailed in paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers low genistin production alone; strongly enhanced by AtMYB12 and dramatically increased when F3H activity is reduced in are mutant background.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic E8:LjIFS expression, selection of highest-genistin line, genetic crosses with AtMYB12 are-/-, and LC-MS quantification of genistein/genistin derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Transgenic expression generated genistin in tomato fruit; levels increased strongly with AtMYB12 and are background.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Competes directly with F3H for naringenin substrate; are mutant strongly enhances isoflavone accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Favorable_Allele: E8:LjIFS transgene in AtMYB12 are-/- background
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Cloned from Lotus japonicus cDNA and expressed under tomato fruit-specific E8 promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Transgenic MicroTom LjIFS lines; selected high-genistin line crossed with AtMYB12 are-/- and F2 segregants analyzed.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit-specific E8 promoter; reduced F3H activity in are background.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: LC-MS/HPLC-MS for genistein, genistin, and diglucosides.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Genistin was the only isoflavonoid detected in LjIFS-only fruit; much higher total isoflavones achieved in AtMYB12 are-/- LjIFS lines.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Reduction in flavonol branch when combined with are mutation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires diversion away from flavonol pathway; may lower endogenous flavonols.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: LjIFS alone produced ~0.3 mg g^-1 DW genistin; with AtMYB12 genistin rose to ~11 mg g^-1 DW; in are-/- lines total isoflavones reached ~78 mg g^-1 DW.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: General tool for biofortification and metabolic engineering of tomato fruit to accumulate high phenylpropanoids, including flavonols, hydroxycinnamates, anthocyanins, stilbenes, and isoflavones.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Fruit-specific when driven by the E8 promoter; analyzed mainly in ripening fruit at 3–10 days after breaker.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes phenylpropanoid accumulation in tomato fruit by activating both flavonoid/phenylpropanoid genes and primary metabolic genes that increase precursor, energy, and reducing power supply.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic fruit-specific overexpression in tomato, RNA-seq, ChIP-qPCR, ChIP-seq, qRT-PCR, isotope feeding/flux analysis, metabolite profiling, and combinatorial crosses with Del/Ros1, VvStSy, and LjIFS.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master positive regulator with multi-level control; unlike Del/Ros1, it increases both demand (secondary pathway genes) and supply (primary metabolism and aromatic amino acid biosynthesis), enabling exceptionally high phenylpropanoid output.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Ripening tomato fruit, especially breaker to 10 days post-breaker; strongest induction observed several days after breaker under E8 promoter control.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_Binding_or_Direct_Target_Evidence: Direct binding shown by ChIP-qPCR and ChIP-seq to promoters of DAHPS, ENO, CHS1, PAL5A/PAL5C/PAL5D, and F3H; bound regions contain TACCTACC motif.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible metabolite repression in photosynthetic tissues discussed; no formal feedback loop experimentally established in fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Heterologous candidate-gene transgenic expression based on known Arabidopsis flavonol regulator; functional characterization in engineered tomato.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Transgenic tomato lines in cv. MicroTom; crosses with Del/Ros1, 35S:VvStSy, LjIFS lines, and are mutant-derived segregants.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Acts synergistically with Del and Ros1 to generate Indigo fruit with elevated anthocyanins, flavonols, and CGA.
Regulation_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage; fruit-specific E8 promoter context.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis and precursor supply for phenylpropanoid metabolism, including glycolysis, pentose phosphate pathway, shikimate pathway, and early flavonoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: LC-MS/HPLC-MS for flavonols, anthocyanins, stilbenes, isoflavones; amino acid LC-MS; 14C and 13C glucose labeling for flux analysis.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Constitutive expression in tobacco reportedly reduced growth, suggesting tissue-context-dependent tradeoffs; in tomato fruit AtMYB12 was especially effective when expressed from E8 promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GEO GSE61014; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE62462
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Reduced major sugars in fruit; increased aromatic amino acids except phenylalanine not necessarily elevated; intense pigmentation and Indigo phenotype in combinatorial line.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible growth reduction under constitutive expression in other species/tissues; broad metabolic reprogramming may alter sugar balance.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DAHPS, ENO, PAL5A, PAL5C, PAL5D, CHS1, F3H; likely additional targets include PGLS, Rpi, ALDO, SUS1, DHQD, SHD, PAT and known flavonoid genes CHI/FLS.
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Previously up to ~10% fruit dry weight as flavonols and hydroxycinnamates; Indigo fruit had ~2-fold more CGA and ~3-fold more flavonols than AtMYB12 alone and ~2-fold more anthocyanins than Del/Ros1; with VvStSy total resveratrol derivatives reached ~5–6 mg g^-1 DW; with LjIFS plus are total isoflavones reached ~78 mg g^-1 DW.
Regulation_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: Tomato mapping/annotation used ITAG2.3 for transcriptome and ChIP analyses
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates genes encoding enzymes of primary metabolism and early/general phenylpropanoid/flavonoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Scope: Multiple pathway genes and upstream precursor supply network
Regulation_Genes.Target_Metabolite_Product: Flavonols, hydroxycinnamates/chlorogenic acid, anthocyanins, stilbenes, isoflavones

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Endogenous tomato homolog useful as a native regulator for peel flavonoid enhancement and potentially metabolic engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Predominantly active in fruit peel, not flesh; assessed in WT fruit at 5 days after breaker.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with elevated expression of primary and flavonoid genes in peel and likely promotes flavonol accumulation similarly to AtMYB12.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Immunodetection, ChIP-qPCR scanning of promoters in WT peel versus flesh, and qRT-PCR correlation of target gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Functional homolog of AtMYB12 in tomato peel that binds the same target motifs and coordinates endogenous flavonoid-associated transcriptional network.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Ripening fruit peel at ~5 days after breaker.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_Binding_or_Direct_Target_Evidence: ChIP-qPCR showed binding to ENO, DAHPS, and CHS1 promoter regions in peel at the same motifs bound by AtMYB12.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Homology/functionally homologous protein to AtMYB12; validated by antibody recognition and endogenous ChIP in tomato.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: WT tomato peel and flesh samples.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Endogenous regulation of flavonol biosynthesis and associated upstream carbon/precursor supply genes in fruit peel.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Inference from gene expression and prior flavonoid accumulation context; no dedicated metabolite assay in this specific figure beyond study-wide LC-MS analyses.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The paper notes support from prior reduced-function y mutant transcriptome studies for SlMYB12-regulated peel flavonoid network.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: NA in direct SlMYB12-specific experiment; study-wide RNA-seq GEO GSE61014 and ChIP-seq GSE62462 focused on AtMYB12.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ENO, DAHPS, CHS1; associated with elevated SlF3H and SlFLS expression in peel.
Regulation_Genes.QTL_or_Locus_Name: y locus discussed indirectly via prior literature, not mapped here
Regulation_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: ITAG2.3 context for tomato genome resources in study
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ENO, DAHPS, CHS1 and likely other flavonol pathway genes in peel.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Scope: Multiple pathway genes and precursor supply network in peel
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fruit peel developmental program during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Variety_or_Cultivar: WT tomato (cultivar not explicitly specified for this assay)

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Can enhance anthocyanin biofortification when combined with Ros1, and synergizes with AtMYB12 for higher anthocyanin-rich fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Active in transgenic ripening tomato fruit in Del/Ros1 line.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit, primarily by activating late anthocyanin biosynthetic genes rather than primary metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic Del/Ros1 fruit phenotype, RT-qPCR of pathway genes, and metabolite profiling of anthocyanins.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive anthocyanin regulator, but less broad than AtMYB12 because it mainly controls demand side of anthocyanin pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Ripening fruit; analyzed around 7 days after breaker.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Transgenic heterologous regulatory gene used previously for anthocyanin engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Del/Ros1 transgenic tomato line and Indigo hybrid with AtMYB12.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Functions with Ros1; synergistic combination with AtMYB12 produces Indigo fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Did not significantly activate primary metabolic pathways or increase aromatic amino acids in fruit, unlike AtMYB12.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Purple/blue-purple fruit coloration in combination lines.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: More limited metabolite scope than AtMYB12; no increase in aromatic amino acid supply.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes, principally late flavonoid/anthocyanin genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Transcription factor (bHLH, from prior knowledge; not explicitly detailed in paper)
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Indigo (with AtMYB12) showed almost twofold more anthocyanins than purple Del/Ros1 alone.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful anthocyanin engineering regulator in tomato fruit, especially in combination with Del and AtMYB12.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Active in transgenic ripening tomato fruit in Del/Ros1 line.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit together with Del, mainly through activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic Del/Ros1 fruit phenotype, RT-qPCR of pathway genes, and metabolite profiling of anthocyanins.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive anthocyanin regulator in Del/Ros1 module; contributes branch-specific activation without major reprogramming of primary metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Ripening fruit; analyzed around 7 days after breaker.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Transgenic heterologous regulatory gene used previously for anthocyanin engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Del/Ros1 transgenic tomato line and Indigo hybrid with AtMYB12.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Functions with Del; synergistic combination with AtMYB12 produces Indigo fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Del/Ros1 did not significantly activate primary metabolic pathways or increase aromatic amino acids compared with AtMYB12 lines.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Purple/blue-purple fruit coloration in combination lines.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Branch-specific activation without broader precursor supply enhancement.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes, principally late flavonoid/anthocyanin genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Transcription factor (MYB, from prior knowledge; not explicitly detailed in paper)
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Indigo (with AtMYB12) showed almost twofold more anthocyanins than purple Del/Ros1 alone.

Title: Multi-level engineering facilitates the production of phenylpropanoid compounds in tomato
